persisted build artifact-ref pages reject linked-output rows outside the dense final artifact slot and reject non-linked rows in that final slot before stale artifact rows can become link-plan output evidence
persisted final linked-output descriptors that consume partial-link records must retain the hierarchical link group identity that proves resumable link state
persisted hierarchical link group pages must match the dense link job slot recorded by the link plan before execution metadata can be prepared

link execution page metadata rejects job slots that precede the link group index before storing partial-link or final-output evidence, so persisted pages cannot imply an impossible dense first-link-job base
persisted hierarchical link execution indexes must match the current link plan dense job and group metadata before resumable execution is trusted
completed link-execution replay requires final-page sidecar interface object and partial-link evidence to exist and match the final execution page job before resumable completion is trusted

completed reduce/final link replay rejects descriptor summary counts that either exceed or drop interface object section symbol unresolved-symbol and relocation evidence carried by consumed partial-link producer execution pages so CPU descriptor metadata cannot stand in for GPU-produced partial-link/object records or missing link-resolution artifacts

completed reduce-link replay requires final-page partial-link input rows to resolve to persisted producer execution pages with matching dense jobs and output keys before resumable completion is trusted
resumable link-execution progress requires the last prepared reduce group partial-link inputs to resolve to persisted producer execution pages before later groups can be prepared
partial-link input key validation treats eight job digits as a minimum width and rejects widened producer job indices with extra leading zeroes before resumable link evidence is trusted
completed reduce-link replay rejects final outputs whose partial-link producer execution pages still carry unbound runtime service requirements until runtime binding evidence exists
live hierarchical link execution rejects paged sidecar inputs that were produced for a different dense link job before streaming those inputs into the linker
